entry.title: Stop query execution in RSRT and AO if the date interval exceeds 90 days.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need to stop the execution of query in RSRT and AO (Analysis for Office) if the given selection date interval exceed ex : 90 days.

Details ::

created 2 variables
1) Serv_Date --> to enter date interval [options --> Interval , Manual input , Mandatory , Ready for input]
2) [Serv_Date_check] --> [options --> customer exit , interval , not enabled for input ] this is used to check Serv_Date interval values.

I have implemented BADI for customer exit variables - calling external program to implement form - endform.

In the RSRT / Analysis for Office selection screen , If the date interval exceeds 90 days it should stop further execution and stay in the selection screen until the given range is below 90 days.
For this I have created a customer exit variable [Serv_Date_check] which will refer the interval given in date variable [Serv_Date].

I am able to throw warning message as popup so that when clicked on New entry button in popup, it allows new interval date in [Serv_Date].
But, if i click ok button in warning popup, it is running for given date range.
In Analysis for office - how to handle this warning or re enter date interval.

I tried to clear the [Serv_Date] variable before throwing error / warning message but query is still executing.
I am unable to stop the query execution.

Please suggest.

Thanks
kv

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning : Advance Formulas (Aggr...
entry.content: MoonJun these are the dimensions product, time, flow, audit, and entity but I have to calculate against northeast only so have applied filter on entity please assist

entry.title: Re: IDOC metadata issue on SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi

concept of SAP is to not unrelease IDOCs once they are released. So you supposed to create a next version. However, this is not very practical, as you have then new Service Interface, OM, etc. 

So in order solve your issue, you have to remove the Meta data from the IDOC adapter's cache

Now with the next data sent by ERP, you receive the payload correctly. For sure, if you want to use the new segment in a graphical map, you have also to re-import the IDOC schema in ESR - for XSLT mapping this don't matter.

BR Helmut

PS: keep it in mind for your go-live in Prod, too 🙂

entry.title: Re: Long bank account number for china vendor (more than...
entry.content: Which version of the system and what type of payment medium are you using? I didn't have to set anything up. There is an option to create your own implementation of badi or exit function ... It depends on which node the DMEE is using.

entry.title: Re: Embedded Intelligent Scenarios in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
hi @AnupT ,

Ideally, predictive models do not require daily training. Regular retraining is necessary to ensure predictions are based on the most recent data. For monthly forecasting scenarios, it's recommended to retrain the model every month when new actuals become available

Predictions can be accessed by anyone with the appropriate permissions, not just the person who initiated the training.

if the data has strange behavior then it will be necessary to create incident and troubleshoot.

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud: Output Parameter Determination - how to get the chanel EMAIL or PRINT
entry.content:
Hi, 
my customer has a problem to get the right channel in the Output Parameter Determination. The request is to get either PRINT or EMAIL as a result. The decision should be made based on the prefered communication channel of the undelying Business Partner.

The fields available in C_BILLINGDOCUMENTOMPARAMDET are not sufficient to achieve this. Adding a field would be an option if the customer would be able to fill it with the needed information.

Any thoughts?

entry.title: Re: Community project: General purpose customizing table
entry.content:
Hi @jrgkraus,

thank you for the project, we have been using a similar approach for years and are currently considering an ABAP Cloud redesign. I haven't searched the sources yet. Can the values ​​be maintained depending on permissions or can anyone maintain everything?

Greetings

Björn

entry.title: Re: PCo Agent status is shown as faulty.
entry.content:
Hi Mohan, yes there is a way to automatically restart them, I called it auto healing. Basically you have to create a new agent instance that monitors all the agent instances you want to restart. I wrote a white paper about this several years ago which I can give you. Reach out to me via email and I can send it (kevin.hunter at sap.com)

Thanks

Kevin

entry.content: Thanks a lot @ TOlschewski, its book is really helpful.

entry.title: Hello sap guru'sWhile posting in fbs1 I got a error that massage number is f5100
entry.content: help me to resolve this

entry.title: Re: **Request for Tools to Create Developer Key**
entry.content:
Hi Kiran, 

Please create an s-user firstly as https://community.sap.com/t5/welcome-corner-blog-posts/how-to-generate-a-new-s-user-id/ba-p/13494625

Then follow the process to request an access key or developer key as stated in 
https://support.sap.com/en/my-support/keys.html?anchorId=section_1619633131

Developer & Object Keys (SSCR)

The SSCR (SAP Software Change Registration) registers all manual changes to SAP sources and SAP Dictionary objects. If an ABAP developer wants to change them in an SAP system, the system will prompt for two access keys:

  • Developer key to register a particular user as a developer. This key must be entered only once.
  • Object key to allow changes to SAP sources or SAP dictionary objects. An object must be registered when it is changed by a registered developer for the first time. If the object is changed at a later point in time, the key is no longer requested.

Requesting keys:

  • Request keys through the SAP Software Change Request (SSCR) Key application.
  • You must have the Register Object and Developer Keys (or at least Register Object Keys) authorization. To request it, please contact your company's super administrator.
  • SAP matchcodes and tuning measures such as the setup of database indices and buffers are excluded from the registration.

Download the help guide for more information.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Access event log, case attributes via API to perform...
entry.content: @hoangvu : Thank you for sharing this article. It is very helpful and related to our use-case. Have you build any other use-case in AP/AR

entry.title: Enhancing Decision-Making with BI Launchpad KPIs
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, quick and informed decision-making is critical. To support this need, our latest BI 2025 release introduces significant improvements to the BI Launchpad by integrating Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) directly onto your homepage. This advancement provides instant visibility of crucial business metrics and report insights, enabling you to make timely and accurate decisions without navigating multiple screens or manually refreshing reports.

What's New?
A Personalized KPI Dashboard
BI Launchpad now features a dedicated area specifically designed to showcase your pinned charts and KPIs. Customize this space effortlessly by clicking the pencil icon and renaming tabs to match your unique workflow.
Creating Tiles: Quick and Easy
You can pin KPI tiles to your BI Launchpad homepage through two convenient methods:

Method 1: Using OpenDocument URLs

Pin any KPI directly by configuring the OpenDocument URL:
1. Go to My Tiles in BI Launchpad.
2. Click (+) to open the Tile Configuration.
3. Provide a clear, concise tile name (up to 50 characters).
4. Set up your OpenDocument URL with key parameters:
     - CUID (Report Identifier)
     - mode=Part
     - sReportPart
     - sType
     - refresh (optional)
     -sInstance(optional)

Getting Latest Data from Reports:
- Fetch the latest report instances automatically by passing Opendocument sInstance(e.g., Last, User, or Param).

5.Add ToolTip for Tiles
6.Validate URLs instantly to ensure accuracy.

Method 2: Directly from Web Intelligence (WebI)
Pin KPIs right from your Web Intelligence documents:
- Reading View: Click the pin icon on the floating toolbar.  

- Design View: Right-click a report part and choose Pin to Home.

Things to Note:
- Documents must be saved first (URL references existing document CUID).
- Tiles automatically capture advanced tooltips from your WebI documents.

Managing Tiles and Instances Effortlessly
Default Tile Limits:
- Each user can pin up to five tiles.
- Administrators can increase this limit up to ten.
Smart URLs and Prompts:
By default, tiles open the latest report instance. However, you can manually customize tile URLs to show specific scheduled instances or prompt-driven reports. If prompt values aren’t pre-set, tiles will conveniently prompt users interactively when opened.
Example Customized URL:

 

http://<YOURSYTEM:PortNumber>/BOE/OpenDocument/opendoc/openDocument.jsp?iDocID=[Your_Report_ID]&mode=Part&sRefresh=N&sInstance=Param&lsMEnter value(s) for Year=2015

 

Keep Your Data Fresh
Tiles come with a convenient refresh option. By default, clicking refresh simply reloads the latest saved instance. If you require fresh data:
- Edit the tile’s URL parameter from sRefresh=N to sRefresh=Y.
- Note that full data refreshes may cause delays—use this wisely.

Useful Tile Actions at Your Fingertips:
- Edit Properties: Adjust settings anytime.
- Go to Document: Directly navigate to your source report.
- Duplicate: Quickly copy existing configurations.
- Refresh: Control when your data updates.
- Maximize: Get an expanded view of your KPI tile, including interactive tooltips.
Make Tiles Your Default Landing Page
Prefer instant access to your KPIs? Set the Tiles Section as your BI Launchpad homepage with these simple steps:
1. Click your User Profile → Settings.
2. Go to Account Preferences.
3. Turn off "Use Administrator Provided Settings".
4. Under Landing Page Preferences, select:
   - Home Page: Custom Group
   - Landing Group: KPI
   - List Documents as: Tile View (Note: List View is not supported).
5. Click Save.

Admin Tips: Managing Tiles Effectively
Set Maximum Tiles:
- Admins can log in to the Central Management Console (CMC) → Applications → BI Launchpad to configure tile limits (up to 10).

Disable Pinning (Optional):
- Uncheck "Enable Pin to Homepage" in the same location if needed.

With these enhancements in the BI 2025 release, BI Launchpad transforms how you interact with data, putting your most crucial insights right at your fingertips.

entry.title: Streamlining SAP BusinessObjects Updates with the Automated Merge Tool
entry.content:
Why Use the Automated Merge Tool?

The Automated Merge Tool streamlines the process of managing configuration files, ensuring minimal manual intervention. It achieves this by automating the backup and merging tasks of configuration files, thus enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of system updates. It is specifically tailored to the needs of BI administrators responsible for managing the Web Tier in the BI Platform.

Streamlining SAP BusinessObjects Updates with the Automated Merge Tool

Maintaining SAP BusinessObjects (BOE) systems, particularly during updates or upgrades, often involves extensive manual processes that can be time-consuming and error-prone. The Automated Merge Tool significantly simplifies this workflow by automating critical tasks related to configuration file management, enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of system updates. It is specifically tailored to the needs of BI administrators responsible for managing the Web Tier in the BI Platform.

Key Functionalities and Workflow:

Automated Backups:

Prior to initiating an update, the Automated Merge Tool performs essential backups, safeguarding configurations such as property files (*.properties), security contexts, and SAML configurations.

Seamless Merging:

Post-update, the tool efficiently merges backed-up configurations with new versions, ensuring consistency and accuracy.

Installation and Initial Setup:

To successfully deploy the Automated Merge Tool:

  1. Establish Connection: Connect remotely to the server hosting the SAP BusinessObjects Web Tier.
  2. Directory Creation: Create a dedicated directory, for example, "AutomatedToolToBackupAndMerge".
  3. Transfer and Unzip: Transfer and unzip the AutomatedMergeTool.zip file into this directory.
  4. Set JAVA_HOME: Configure the JAVA_HOME environment variable to point to your bundled SAP JVM.

Detailed Configuration and Customization:

Configuration settings are managed through the AppConfig.xml file located within the "ConfigFiles" folder. Through this configuration file, administrators can:

  • Define BOE installation paths.
  • Enable or disable plugins selectively based on operational requirements.

Plug-in Architecture and Functionalities:

The Automated Merge Tool leverages a plugin mechanism, providing flexibility and specific task management:

  • boe.custom.properties: Manages backup for all custom property files but does not support merging.
  • boe.securityContextFile & biprws.securityContextFile: Handle backups and merges for the Tomcat SecurityContext.xml files for BOE and biprws web applications, respectively.
  • boe.saml.config.files & biprws.saml.config.files: Handle backups and restoration of SAML configuration files including XML and keystore files.
  • dswsbobje.webservices.axis: Responsible for handling backups and merging specifically for Axis configurations.
  • dswsbobje.properties: Responsible for handling backups and merging for dsws properties.
  • fioriBI.custom.css: Specifically manages backups and restoration of Theme designer files.

Plugin-Based Flexibility:

The tool operates based on a modular plugin architecture, each designed for specific tasks such as backup, merge, copy, and restore. Administrators can enable or disable individual plugins via AppConfig.xml to precisely align with their update requirements.

Execution Commands:

  • Backup Command: Creates a backup and copies files to specified locations and the tool’s backup folder.
java -jar AutomatedMergeTool.jar backup <log_level>
  • Merge Command: Merges configurations post-update to ensure integration of customized settings.
java -jar AutomatedMergeTool.jar merge <log_level>

Logging and Debugging:

Detailed logs can be generated with various verbosity levels (INFO, DEBUG, WARN, etc.) to aid troubleshooting and auditing.

Availability:

The Automated Merge Tool is conveniently accessible within SAP BusinessObjects official resources, specifically within the Collateral folder provided during installation.

Important Usage Guidelines:

  • Always execute the Backup command before updates to secure configuration files.
  • Avoid deleting crucial files such as lastBackupTakenTime.properties, essential for the merge operation.

Conclusion:

The Automated Merge Tool significantly enhances the efficiency, reliability, and accuracy of SAP BusinessObjects system updates by automating critical backup and merging tasks, minimizing downtime, and reducing manual effort.

entry.title: Re: Import multiple bpmn to SAP Signavio
entry.content: Yes, one by one via the import but depending your case, our Consulting Services can support with larger projects. Note, this is a charged service.

entry.title: How to install flow function "Set component styles"?
entry.content:
Documentation mentions a flow function "Set component styles". I am not able to locate this in the marketplace. How do I install this? 

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/references/set-component-styles?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Join in DataSphere
entry.content:
@Ganjos 

For inner join to work the joining keys should have matching data. So the key column should be having common data for the 2 dataset to get joined. If your keys have totally different data then there is no match and nothing will be returned. So examine your 2 datasets and ensure you create columns which are identical and has matching data. You could create calculated column to create join key columns if required.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Problem in SUM - PREP_INPUT_CHECK/READDATA_EXP - Upgrade SAP BW System
entry.content:
Hello Support,

we want to upgrade our SAP BW System T02 to Netweaver 750 and current sp-Stack - see attached Screenshot.

In SUM we got in PREP_INPUT_CHECK/READDATA_EXP the error 

Could not find suitable DVDs for product NW required for the procedure.

The requested data carrier is not mounted on any of the mount points you entered.

Enter at least the mount point containing Export DVD for product NW.

I found note 2957413 - Incorrect DVD number in SUM phase: PREP_INPUT_CHECK/READDATA_EXP 

So I think I had to change configuration in Maintenance Planner - but I am not sure.

Can you help me out?

Best Regards

Christoph Greiner

entry.title: Customer course UX100 (SAP Fiori – Foundation) available for SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
The course development of the UX100 is (again) finished. Let’s summarize all the new and updated topics in a central place. Welcome to my fourth blog post about the customer training UX100 (SAP Fiori – Foundation).

If you are more interested in a digital learning for SAP Fiori, please read my blog post:
SAP Learning Journey – Learning the Basics of SAP Fiori

Introduction

Starting in 2016, this is now the seventh incarnation of the introduction to SAP Fiori for on-premise. SAP Fiori moved on and therefore the UX100 moved on. With SAP S/4HANA 2023, the SAP Fiori groups are deprecated and were removed from UX100. In addition, the SAP Fiori launchpad designer is no longer recommended and was reduced to its core tasks, where no other tool is in place, yet. Finally, the appendix was dropped with all those long-ago-features from the SAP Business Suite.

The units in the current version are the following:

  1. End-User Perspective
  2. Technology
  3. Architecture
  4. Content Management
  5. Content Administration
  6. Adaptation
  7. Mobility
  8. Integration
  9. Further Information

If you are interested in an adapted version of the material, you can order a customer specific version of the course including only the topics you wish.

System Landscape

Let’s start with the system release: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02. Our system landscape – we call it universal target – is used in nearly all our technology courses and offers full access for the participants from the SAP Fiori launchpad (FLP) in the browser down to SAP HANA on the SUSE Linux Enterprise server (SLES). Everything is set up following the newest guidelines of SAP so that it can really act as a template for customers.

UX100 System Landscape (Screenshot from System Setup Guide)

In addition, we offer a self-service for participants to create a subaccount in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) for the exercises. It is comparable to the SAP BTP trial but limited to services used in UX100 and only valid for the training week. If you need more time to test SAP BTP, please check out the SAP BTP trial.

User Experience

One goal of this update was to update all slides to the newest design guidelines of SAP. Nowhere can this be seen better as in the SAP Fiori launchpad itself. SAP Fiori with Horizon provides a fresh look and feel with new colors and a better user guidance.

How to Add Apps as Favorite to My Home Page (Screenshot from UX100)

The first thing users see when starting the SAP Fiori launchpad is the new My Home. It is a page just for the user, which can be personalized in many ways. Beside to dos, pages, and insights, the apps section can be populated with favorites. This can be done in two ways:

  1. Choose Add Apps in the Apps section to open the app finder and select an app from a catalog.
  2. On an SAP Fiori page assigned to your user in FLP, choose Edit Current Page in the User Actions Menu and choose Add to My Home in the context menu of a tile.

The first way comes in handy if you browse all apps assigned to you without concerning, if the app is already part of any page. It is a search for functionality rather than process. The second way instead allows to bring an app in the foreground, which may be the start of a bigger process the user regularly runs from a page. It is about getting faster access to this process.

For more information about the SAP Fiori user experience, please read the blog post of ThomasReiss:
User Experience Advances with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (Private Cloud and On-Premise)

Technology

The other ongoing goal is to keep track with all the advancements in SAP Fiori technologies. For example, TypeScript got introduced in SAPUI5 to ease the work of developers and consuming OData V4 services gets more and more common. The development tools continue to advance, too.

How to Access a Dev Space in Visual Studio Code (Screenshot from UX100)

The SAP Business Application Studio toolkit extension allows to connect and access dev spaces of SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) in Visual Studio (VS) Code. It can be found and installed in the extension marketplace.

The extension adds the SAP BAS Desktop Client as side panel. For the connection, the URL to access the SAP BAS is needed. The logon uses a Web browser window asking for your credentials and establishing an SSH connection in a new VS Code window. You can access and change the connection details choosing the blue SSH URL at the bottom of the window.

Accessing a dev space in VS Code loads all extensions of the dev space in the local VS Code window. This includes extensions, which are not available in the extension marketplace like Joule. In addition, you can add additional extensions from the extension marketplace.

You can start and stop dev spaces, open the dev space manager, and connect to multiple dev spaces of different accounts. All settings are saved in the dev spaces but are used locally in VS Code.

For more information about the SAP BAS in VS Code, please read the blog post of geert-janklaps:
Access SAP Business Application Studio as a remote from Visual Studio Code

Spaces and Pages

The heart of the UX100 will always be the content management of the SAP Fiori launchpad. One part of this is spaces and pages. Introduced in SAP S/4HANA 2020, they got some new features in the latest releases like sort priority or merging spaces and pages with other spaces and pages.

Merge of SAP Fiori Pages (Screenshot from UX100)

Sometimes users see the same apps several times or have similar pages assigned more than once, because they have similar roles assigned. For example, the sales manager and the sales accountant have overlapping tasks and therefore apps in their pages. Such pages can be merged to combine the apps or sections in one transient page at runtime. This means that the merged page is not visible in the Manage Launchpad Pages app, but just for the user in the SAP Fiori launchpad having all pages assigned.

Customer pages can be merged with other customer pages or with an SAP page. For the second one, you enter the name of the SAP page in the Merge ID field in the General Data tab of the customer page. When merging customer pages, you can define an own name for the merged page and enter it as Merge ID in all pages you want to merge.

For more information about merging spaces and pages, please read the blog post of KattiaJ-P:
Merging Spaces and Pages

Catalog Management

The other part of SAP Fiori content is catalogs. There were several advances in the latest releases, for example, the introduction of typed catalogs in SAP S/4HANA 2020. The tool for managing those catalogs is the SAP Fiori launchpad application manager (FLPAM). In SAP S/4HANA 2023, it got a new feature.

How to Adapt a Technical Catalog (Screenshot from UX100)

The adaptation mode in the FLPAM allows you to make (limited) adaptations in the launchpad app descriptor items (LADI) of SAP-delivered technical catalogs. The changes are directly available in all references in business catalogs.

Switching to the adaptation mode adds the Adaptation tab to the details of a LADI. Enlarge the tab and choose Edit: You can now select the Adapt checkbox for each property you want and enter your adaptation in the input field behind. Adapted values can be translated using the SE63.

For more information about adapting technical catalogs, please read the blog post of ruth_groene:
SAP Fiori Launchpad: New Fast and Easy Option to Adapt SAP-delivered Launchpad Content (for On-Premise and Cloud, Private Edition)

Key User Adaptation

There are so many other topics around SAP Fiori that there is just not enough room for everything in a 5-day-couse. But one topic was always there as an outlook: Key user adaptation. With key user adaptation or UI adaptation at runtime (RTA), SAP Fiori apps based on SAPUI5 that support RTA can be changed directly and intuitively in the SAP Fiori launchpad without developing any source code. With SAP S/4HANA 2023, versions of such app variants were introduced.

How to Activate and Publish a New Version (Screenshot from UX100)

After the adaptations to an app have been saved in a variant, they can be activated as a new version. This sets this variant as the active version for all users in the system replacing the original app or any previously active version. You can switch to an older version by activating it as a new version.

With the active version being selected, the Publish button can be used to assign it to a transport request. A customizing or workbench request can be selected to decide, if the adaptations should be available client-specific or cross-client in the follow-up system.

For more information about key user adaptation, please read the tutorial of Vitor Seifert:
Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 Applications - UI Changes and Modes

Summary

If you have read so far, it seems that you are really thinking about attending the course. Don’t hesitate and check out the physical and virtual events we offer:

https://training.sap.com/course/UX100

If you are more interested in a digital learning, please visit https://learning.sap.com. Many parts of the UX100 are already available free-of-charge:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journey/learn-the-basics-of-sap-fiori

For any other questions around the offerings of SAP Learning Services, please jump to our community page and get in contact with us:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/training-certification

I hope even if you won’t attend the UX100 in any kind, you found some useful information or references around SAP Fiori in this blog post.

Happy learning
Stefan

entry.title: Re: Reimagining digital learning as an integrated part of a Digital Adoption Platform with SAP &&&&
entry.content:
@AndrewCassidy 

Hi Andrew, 

while the scope of features for the new WalkMe offering is not completely defined yet and will of course evolve, I can confirm that digital learning in the sense of a canvas based content creation feature will be included. This will come in addition to the transactional recording and AI-based content creation plus analytics.

entry.title: Re: How to use create condition contract when the conditi...

I have tried using product hierarchy full field expecting it to update product hierarchy 2. But that approach does not work. I have mentioned extending BAPI_TE_KOMG with product hierarchy 2. I get an error that PRODH2 is not a SAP supported key field. So both approaches did not work for me

entry.title: Re: Long bank account number for china vendor (more than...
entry.content: We are using S4 HANA version and paym/functional module related payment medium we are using and I have checked existing file where bank acoount number is triggering which is under debt acct --ID-- IBAN

entry.title: SAP C4C Custom Object Volume Checks and Validations
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have a requirement where we need to bring the product stock information from SAP B1 to SAP C4C. As we need to get the details of the product like below

Product 

  • Warehouse
    • Bin
      • Batch
        • Quantity

After considering all the standard solutions we concluded that we have to go with a custom solution of creating the business object and replicate the data from SAP B1.

Below is the proposed structure of custom BO:

  1. Root Node
    • Child Node (First child)
      • Child Node (Second child)
        • Child Node (Third child)
          • Child Node (Fourth child)

We are going to define the BODL of the customer BO as per the above design and the records in the BO will exceed more than 10 million.

We wanted to know about SAP standard limitations as per below points

  • Can we define such kind of complex custom BO 
  • Is there any limit for the number of records/data?
  • Since we are going to use custom BO to replicate the data from SAP B1, do we have any limitations for this kind of complex structure?
  • Since we are going to use this custom BO to integrate the data while building the API or WSDL which option is suggested by SAP whether SOAP method or REST method?
  • Once the custom BO has data, do we have any issue while retrieving the data on the screen level (seeing the data on the OWL screen)?

Below is the field names and the data types we are going to be defined

Sno

Field Name

Data Type

1

Product ID

ID

2

Product Name

Medium text (80 char)

3

Ware house

ID

4

Ware house Name

Medium text (80 char)

5

Bin ID

ID

6

Bin Name

Medium text (80 char)

7

Batch ID

ID

8

Batch Name

Medium text (80 char)

9

Quantity

Quantity/Numeric

10

UOM

Unit of Measurement

11

Stock (Quantity)

Quantity/Numeric

12

Committed Stock (Quantity)

Quantity/Numeric

13

Available Stock (Quantity)

Quantity/Numeric

14

Ordered Stock (Quantity)

Quantity/Numeric

15

Category

Medium text (80 char)

16

Sub Category

Medium text (80 char)

17

Average Price

Currency

18

8% - Price

Currency

19

10% - Price

Currency

20

12% - Price

Currency

21

15% - Price

Currency

22

In (MT)

Weight

23

Weight (Inventory)

Weight

Kindly please suggest as the SAP C4C will support us with the above mentioned details

Considering the above information, let me know if c4c standard can cater our requirement in a standard way.

 

Thanks in Advance,

Best Regards,
Navaneeth Kumar.D

entry.title: Re: BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_CREATE - Getting error and can't find field
entry.content: you can't use negative value on your BAPI or it will geting error. I was facing same error when my tax_amount negative.

entry.title: Re: Upload Customer Open Items (Fiori App F4051) --> Miss...
entry.content: Hi, Thank you for letting me know that I am not alone in this. 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - Renaming Apps
entry.content:
Hi,

It sounds like something is obstructing the size limit error. This would still prevent your import if it displayed correctly.

240MB sounds curiously large to me for a project size. How many pages does your app have, and are you storing lots of static data (e.g. images) inside the app which could be fetched over the network?

BR,
Pekka

entry.title: Re: Enhancing IDoc Filtering in SAP BD64
entry.content: Great Work! 👏

entry.title: Re: Implementing and Debugging User Exits: Function Module in SAP Sales Order (VA01)
entry.content: thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: Enhancing IDoc Filtering in SAP BD64
entry.content: Informative one!👏

entry.title: SAP ERP公有云预算和计划功能 - 手工导入计划收入数据
entry.content:
背景

很多公有云的实施商和客户有SAP ECC 的实施和使用经验。SAP ECC有一些计划的功能比如：SOP(Sales and operation planning)的功能，基于成本获利能力分析的收入计划功能，成本中心费用计划和通过活动类型计划的消耗数量进一步计算活动类型的计划价格等等。这些功能在S/4HANA 私有云中依然可用（默认处于非激活的状态），但在公有云中这些功能已经不存在了，相关的功能需要在SAP Analytics Cloud和Business Integration Planning这两个SAP产品中实现，或者在非SAP的计划工具软件中实现，然后把计划数据传入S/4HANA Cloud中。

有三种方式可以在S/4HANA系统中生成计划数据

  1. 使用 Fiori 应用导入财务计划数据通过Excel模板导入
  2. 使用Financial Plan Data-Write API
  3. 集成SAC 计划

系统操作

本系列博客将陆续介绍SAP产品的计划功能和S/4HANA公有云的集成。本文将介绍第一种方式通过Excel模板导入计划数据到S/4HANA系统中

打开Fiori 应用导入财务计划数据

可以看到有多个模板

  • 成本中心计划模板用来导入成本中心费用数据，计划的数据后续可以通过Fiori应用管理分配在成本中心中进行分配。
  • 成本中心预算编制模板用来导入成本中心预算数据，后续使用Fiori应用成本中心预算报表查看成本中心的实际和预算的数据
  • 市场细分计划模板用来导入计划的收入。在下载模板的基础之上，可以添加更多的标准字段和扩展字段。参考Help Portal文档：用于财务计划数据导入的字段(此功能类似于在ECC时代基于成本获利能力分析输入或导入计划收入的功能)

后面我们介绍一下市场细分计划模板导入计划收入的流程。

假设我们在SAP系统外已经做好了未来的销售计划，计划数据已经转换成导入模板的格式，在默认导入模板中填加了利润中心，获利能力产品，以公司代码货币计的金额，公司代码货币字段

Fiori 应用导入财务计划数据 点击浏览CSV文件，选择已经准备好的上传文件。可以先测试导入源文件，确定没有报错并且显示将要导入的数据和Excel中准备的数据相符。点击导入源文件。计划数据成功导入后可以点击显示财务计划数据。

也可以使用Fiori 应用市场细分-计划和实际查看收入的计划和实际的数据。

计划和实际的数据通过CDS View I_ActualPlanJrnlEntryItemCube和I_ActualPlanLineItemSemTag查看，可以用来进一步制作嵌入式分析的报表。

小结

本文概览的介绍了计划数据在S/4HANA公有云系统中生成的方式，详细介绍了Excel导入计划收入数据的过程和报表的查看方法，后续博客还会介绍SAP产品其他的计划功能。欢迎关注和讨论，并查看其他的论坛博客。

entry.title: Re: Email Notifications in SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation
entry.content:
Hi @annelies_stern,

If you are the responsible for a task you should get the notification in case someone else is adding a comment to the task.

  • It is not working for involved parties.
  • And the comment must be added by another user.

There are no additional settings needed.

Best regards,

Moritz

entry.title: Pass Grant ID and Fund to I_JOURNALENTRYTP for posting
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have developed an custom application using SAP RAP model in SAP Public Cloud.

How can I pass grant id and fund id to I_JOURNALENTRYTP for posting FI document?

Thank you!

entry.title: TOP-DOWN
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Expenses in the cost place need to be allocated to profitability. Recipient = material, distribution criteria = sales quantity. How should I configure the Sender and Receiver basis? Can you please help me with this in detail.

Thanks for your help.

entry.title: Custom Condition Not Displayed in Supplier Invoice Item
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I created a custom condition (Z002) in "Define Condition Types."

After that, I added this new condition along with the standard freight condition (FQU1) to the PO.

However, when creating the supplier invoice, only the standard freight condition (FQU1) are displayed, while the custom condition (Z002) does not appear independently.

Did I miss any configuration steps? Any insights would be greatly appreciated. Thanks!

please come up with suitable solution.
#BPA #Ui5 #automation #BTP #Fiori #SAP

Best regards
Kushagra Sharma

entry.title: Re: Configure webhook notifications from SAP Cloud ALM to SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content: Nice to know that this is possible. However, if possible, it might be best to send it to some form of message broker, e.g. Event Mesh capability or SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, so that the event can be easily distributed to many more consumers in case it is needed in the future.,

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management 

Please suggest.

Thank you very much.Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Fiori-Style Cockpit/Pervasive Analytics
entry.content: Did you resolve this issue? I have the same issue.

entry.title: Re: Syntax error in program SAPLWR11
entry.content:
You need to open an incident in the application component shown in the dump header. It's not a SAP GUI issue.

Check the basis level on your system (SAPKBXXXX) to see if there are any notes currently addressing your issue.

entry.title: SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud: Direct Transfer or Staging Tables?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am new to data migration and currently exploring the best way to transfer data from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. Traditionally, migrations involve staging tables as an intermediate step, but I’m wondering whether a direct transfer is possible and, if so, whether it is the preferred approach.

Here are some key questions I am trying to answer:

 

Technical Requirements:

  • What are the key prerequisites for migrating data directly from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Are there specific configurations required on the ECC side?

 

Connectivity: RFC or Other Methods?

  • Can this be achieved through an RFC connection between SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  • If not, what alternative connectivity options exist?

 

Migration Approach: Direct Transfer vs. Staging Tables

  • Is a direct data transfer from SAP ECC to SAP Public Cloud even possible?
  • Or is it better to extract the data into staging tables before loading it into the cloud system?
  • What are the advantages and disadvantages of both approaches?

 

Experience & Recommendations

  • Has anyone here worked on such a migration before?
  • What were your biggest challenges?
  • Would you recommend direct transfer, or is a staging approach more reliable?

 

I’d love to hear from the community—any best practices, lessons learned, or technical insights would be greatly appreciated! 

Looking forward to your thoughts! 😊

Best,
Burak

entry.title: Re: Unable to modify Sales BOM
entry.content: Hello Everyone, I am also facing a similar issue. I am trying to replace a Component in a Sales BoM with a new one. When I checked yes, the main BoM has entry in EKPO and also the Component that I am trying to replace also exist in VBPA table where GBSTA <> C. Can someone tell me what are the next step. I checked the SAP Note SAP Message no. /BEV1/NE009, however it says to use CS07 or CS08, but those transactions are for Plant Assignment. My requirement is to change the Sales BoM Component for the same Plant.

entry.title: Re: Feasibility of OEE KPI in Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi Utkarsh,

For all the requirements mentioned above, you will have to create custom manage dashboards. In the standard OEE report it has limited use. 
According to me one should should design their own dashboard and also use own calculations, if needed, from the available MDOs.

KR,
Say

entry.title: Re: Create PO from PR by Process Purchase Requisitions (V2)

entry.title: Re: How to Print Comments in the TAB Console in Eclipse f...
entry.content: Thanks. So, chapter 5, in short: in the code insert calls to cl_ble_trace_writer=>write_info_message, run the app "Custom Logic Tracing", start the trace, run the BAdI, stop and display the trace.

Regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA BP screen enhancement with custom tab
entry.content:
Hello Maria,

unfortunately I don't know the possibility to extend BUT050.
From my experience this is the first time I hear a customer wants to extend Relationship. I think this is the reason why standard development does not support such extension.

Thinking a bit further in SAP S/4HANA extensibility with clean core approach. If you find a workaround for your requirement, this will not supported by Clean Core approach.

Sorry for the bad news.
Best Regards
Andi

What is the correct way to do this? It seems that even when passing the highest sequential number, the value is always forced to 1.

Thanks in advance

Giulian

entry.title: Re: Unable to download Desktop Agent for SAP Build Proces...
entry.content: Could you double-check your SAP for Me>My setting>Authorizations&Functions page? In order to download Desktop Agent, your also need to be assigned to "Software Download" role. If you are not, contact your co-worker who manage atuhentication for SAP for Me. Regards,

entry.title: Re: REDUCE not concatenating strings
entry.content: You can achieve the same by sing backticks ` instead of single quotes '.

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Sandra Solís
entry.content:
Amazing to see the spirit of Blog It Forward live after 13 years!

Thanks a lot and I enjoyed reading your BIF:).

entry.title: Re: Email notification when manager initiates Onboarding...
entry.content:
Hi Antony,

Scenarios

For scenario 1 (initiate onboarding trigger) you may need to create your own trigger and email category. You can follow KBA 3457194 - Custom Rehire Verification Notification Configuration - Onboarding, but change the Process Task Type to 'Review New Hire Data'. 

For scenario 2 (complete RNHD step), you can copy the standard External User Welcome Message Template'. Do not deactivate the original when requested and rework the copy to your requirement. This will always trigger when PDC triggers (RNHD completion).

Tokens:

If the desired tokens are not available, you can create your own - refer to this community blog.

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-members/custom-tokens-for-onboarding-2-0-email-notifications/ba-p/13560942

Be aware that custom tokens may not work when you do not use 'Provide Additional Data' when you manually initiate onboarding as the relevant position details will not be available for the system to resolve the tokens.

Hope this helps.

Regards

Frans

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning : Advance Formulas (Aggr...
entry.content:
Hi @SANA11

Please refer to the below script. 

It seems that aggregating only the DATE dimension would be sufficient to achieve the desired result, but the Audit and Flow dimensions were also aggregated.

MEMBERSET [d/Entity] = "REG0001"

VARIABLEMEMBER #All_Product OF [d/PRODUCT]

//Aggregate "Units" & "Lookup_Price" of Produdt, Flow, Audit and Date members and write it on VARIABLEMEMBER #All_Product
DATA([d/PRODUCT] = #All_Product, [d/Date] = "201801", [d/FLOW] = "#", [d/AUDIT] = "#") = RESULTLOOKUP()

//Calculate Revenue to multiply Aggregated "Units" & "Lookup_Price" of Product and write it on Product "#" and Account "Revenue"
DATA([d/PRODUCT] = "#", [d/Date] = "201801", [d/FLOW] = "#", [d/AUDIT] = "#", [d/Account] = "DRV0060") =
  RESULTLOOKUP([d/PRODUCT] = #All_Product, [d/Date] = "201801", [d/FLOW] = "#", [d/AUDIT] = "#", [d/Account] = "DRV0020") *
  RESULTLOOKUP([d/PRODUCT] = #All_Product, [d/Date] = "201801", [d/FLOW] = "#", [d/AUDIT] = "#", [d/Account] = "DRV0055")

Regards,

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Data Migration: Tool to fill the XML Excel-Templates ?

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere and SAP Information Steward Connectivity
entry.content: In IS, there is no specific connection type for Datasphere. If Datasphere has server name like normal ECC system, you can try with SAP Application connection type.

entry.title: Supply Task Finish Control
entry.content:
In supply task, is it possible to set a hard check that only when all input products have been supplied completely can the task be finished.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: No projects available for my Desktop Agent
entry.content:
Dear community,

Does anyone know how to resolve this issue: "It looks like no project has been received yet.

When there are, you'll see them here." on Desktop Agent?

I got this issue although I already set up enough settings as below:

①Project : deployed to the environment "public"

②Agent : added to the environment "public"

③Desktop Agent : Attended mode

④Connection between Desktop Agent & Tenant : Active

I have no idea about what I should do anymore. I would appreciate it if you gave me some setting that I miss, some advice on this, etc..

Thank you.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Supply Task Finish Control
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

ByD doesn't support this currently. This is a flexibility that system provides for users to finish task any time, no matter how many input products are supplied.

Please consider creating add-on to fulfill your requirement.

Kind Regards,

Henry

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Project Control - Currency
entry.content:
Hi Sanne,

sorry for the late reply and thank you for the request 

Yes, when you create an enterprise project, by default, the system will derive the currency from the company code which is derived from the cost center maintained.

Regarding the Project Brief, yes only the global currency is shown and it works as designated.

KR,

Omar

entry.title: Comprehensive Overview: SCIM API for SAP Datasphere User Management
entry.content:
Introduction

In Q3 2024, SAP introduced the SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) API for SAP Datasphere to enhance user management through a consistent, REST-based protocol. This API currently supports the following operations:

  • CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations for users
  • Assign users to existing roles (Note: Creating new roles via API is not supported, as mentioned in the documentation)
  • List all users
  • Retrieve API information (e.g., identity provider, available schemas, resource types)

This blog provides a detailed overview, complementing the official . Most of the examples referenced are consistent with the SAP documentation examples. Next to the documentation, the aim is to give a comprehensive overview with examples and provide a Postman collection for testing on your own.

In this Repository you can find a Postman collection to test the SCIM API.

Setup

The first step is to create an OAuth client in your SAP Datasphere tenant with the appropriate settings. Make sure to note down the Client ID and Client Secret for token retrieval.

You will also need the Token URL from the App Integration section.

Login and CSRF Token Retrieval

To obtain the bearer token, make a POST or GET request to the following endpoint:

https://<token_url>/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials

The request will then look like this:

  • Authorization: Use Basic Authentication with the OAuth Client ID as the username and the OAuth Client Secret as the password, both of which were obtained when creating the OAuth client.
  • Response: Upon a successful request, the response will contain the access token.

In the response we will then receive the Access Token:

Once you have obtained the access token, you can request the CSRF token by sending a GET request to the following endpoint:

https://<tenant_url>/api/v1/csrf

The request will look like this:

The request must include the following headers:

  • Authorization: Bearer <Access_Token> (the token obtained in the previous step)
  • x-sap-sac-custom-auth: true
  • x-csrf-token: fetch

The CSRF token can be found in the response header under the x-csrf-token key.

CRUD Operations

For all subsequent API requests, it is essential to include the Bearer token and the x-sap-sac-custom-auth: true header in the request.  See the header example below:

Additionally, for any PUT, POST, PATCH, or DELETE requests, you must include the retrieved CSRF token in the request header as x-csrf-token: <token>. See the header with the x-csrf-token below:

GET Users and Individual User

To get a list of all users or information about a specific user, you can use the following endpoints:

https://<tenant_url>/api/v1/scim2/Users

https://<tenant_url>/api/v1/scim2/Users/<user ID>

The response for an individual user looks like this:

Create User

To create a new user, send a POST request to the following endpoint:

https://<tenant_URL>/api/v1/scim2/Users/

In the request payload, you must provide the user details. You can also assign roles to the user at the time of creation, as shown in the sample payload below:

Note: This example is taken from the SAP documentation for consistency.

Modify User

To modify an existing user, use either a PUT or a PATCH request:

  • PUT: This request will overwrite all properties of the user with the values specified in the request payload.
  • PATCH: This request updates only the specified properties, leaving the rest unchanged.

The endpoint for user modification is as follows:

https://<tenant_url>/api/v1/scim2/Users/<user ID>

Delete User

To delete a user, you need to send a DELETE request to the following endpoint, providing the user's ID:

https://<tenant_url>/api/v1/scim2/Users/<user ID>

You can retrieve the user ID by performing a GET request to list all users.

Bulk Operations

The SCIM API also supports bulk operations, allowing you to perform multiple actions in a single request. To perform a bulk operation, send a POST request to the following endpoint:

https://<tenant_URL>/api/v1/scim2/Bulk/

For example, you can create multiple users with one API call by specifying the details in the request payload. A detailed example of a bulk operation payload can be found in the official SAP Documentation.

Conclusion

The SCIM API for SAP Datasphere enables efficient user management through a REST-based protocol, supporting CRUD operations, role assignments, and bulk actions. This blog provides an in-depth overview of the API endpoints and usage, complementing the official SAP documentation. For more detailed examples and use cases, the SAP documentation remains an invaluable resource.

entry.title: Re: Project Control - Currency
entry.content:
Hi Sanne,

thank you for the request. Yes, when you create an enterprise project, by default, the system will derive the currency from the company code which is derived from the cost center maintained.

Regarding the Project Brief, yes only the global currency is shown and it works as designated.

KR,

Omar

entry.title: [PUBLIC]SAP Open Source Webinar: Apeiro Reference Architecture – Strengthening Digital Sovereignty
entry.content:
Europe is investing in innovative cloud solutions which harmonize regulatory frameworks to build a resilient, secure digital future with its IPCEI-CIS initiative around next generation cloud infrastructures and services. The Apeiro Reference Architecture (ApeiroRA) is part of this initiative and aims to build a high-performance cloud-edge infrastructure with open-source standards.
Join us on May 14th at 3:00 pm CEST, 6:30 pm IST for a public webinar about ApeiroRA and the new NeoNephos Foundation. Peter Giese, Head of the SAP Open Source Program Office will give you an overview why and how this project has been transferred into the new NeoNephos Foundation, which will serve as a neutral entity dedicated to fostering open collaboration and governance. Vasu Chandrasekhara, Chief Product Owner of the Apeiro Project will explain the landscape of Apeiro RA with an overview on the architecture and components. Learn and discuss, which software and data sharing tools build up this European cloud-edge continuum and get a glimpse into some of its functionality.
Speakers: Peter Giese, Head of the SAP Open Source Program Office and Vasu Chandrasekhara, Chief Product Owner of the Apeiro Project, SAP
Host: SAP Open Source Program Office

Register now!

entry.title: Re: SolMan - incident status set to in process when RFC s...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

You can change this behaviour by going to SM34 => AIC_SETTINGS2 

In SPRO, you get there with path:

SAP Solution Manager 

> Capabilities (Optional)

  > Change Control Management 

   > Change Request Management Framework

    > Make settings for change transaction types

 

Then, select your request for change transaction type , navigate to folder "assign actions", and check the actions for user status E0005 (this is the usual for Implemented) and for E0006 (confirmed). 

The action SET_PREDOC is the action that changes the incident status. You may remove this action from your request for change transaction type .

If you remove this setting from standard transaction types such as SMCR, any piece list activation after a Support Package update will revert the configuration, so it is always a good practice to have your own Z transaction types in ChaRM.

 

Best regards, 

 

Miguel Ariño

entry.title: ADT Quick Assist Error
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I get following error when using the QuickAssist (ctrl + 1) for creating literals in the text pool. Everything else works fine (renaming, constructor creation) only the literals creation/adding will give me the following error:

Cannot invoke "org.eclipse.jface.text.quickassist.IQuickAssistAssistant.getQuickAssistProcessor()" because the return value of "org.eclipse.jface.text.source.ISourceViewerExtension3.getQuickAssistAssistant()" is null

The text pool and literal are created, but the error can't be closed, which forces me to force close the entire eclipse.
Any ideas?

Eclipse Version: 2024-03 (4.31.0)
SAP GUI for Java Embedding Version : Thu Oct 17 10:18:04 CEST 2019 (1771811)
Testing on the SAP System D82

entry.title: #CoE#LAL Cannot create sales order with customer down payment when this order contains free goods.
entry.content: In VA01，Cannot create sales order with customer down payment when this order contains free goods. If this order has contained free goods (TANN), then cannot set the item category to CBAO. Is this the standard setting? Can we change the configuration?

entry.title: How to implement additional restrictions for users creating Sales Orders
entry.content:
If you want to restrict users to create sales orders, you can use the provided restriction types in the business catalog (see help portal). 

If that types are not sufficient for your use case, you can implement additional checks using key user extensibility. In this blog, we will use key user extensibility to restrict users for specific plants and give a demo for this function.

For this function, we create the following Custom Business Object in the Custom Business Object app:

- YY1_PLANTRESTRICTION: This business object could set the restrictions for plants for different user.

 

In this Custom Business Object, in the subpage General Information, we need to select some attributes to meet our requirement. For more details, you could refer to Custom Business Objects | SAP Help Portal.

In the subpage Fields, we will add two fields called Username and Plant.

For these two fields, we use Association to CDS View as a type. With that we get a value help for available users and plants in the system.

For field Username, we use value help view I_WORKFORCEPERSONVH.

For field Plant, we use value help view I_PLANTSTDVH.

After maintaining fields, we could publish this Custom Business Object for the next step. And then maintain data in “Maintain Catalogs” from subpage General Information. If you do not maintain any catalogs, the “Generated UI” will not enable to be opened for lacking of authority.

For our example, we maintain this catalog (Extensibility – Custom Business Objects) for Business Object (PlantRestriction). Then the user who own this catalog could be able to maintain the value for Business Object "PlantRestriction".

After maintaining the catalog, you can navigate to the generated UI from subpage General Information.

In this UI, you could create, update and delete any user and plant restriction you like. Here I configured two users’ restriction. User Wesley can only use plant 1010 and Yilin can only use plant 1710.

Then we need to implement the cloud BADI SD_SLS_CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE to prevent mentioned user to use the restriction plant.

 

 

IF salesdocument-purchaseorderbycustomer = 'Plant Check'.
    DATA(lv_actual_user_alias) = cl_abap_context_info=>get_user_alias(  ).

    LOOP AT salesdocumentitems INTO DATA(lv_salesdocumentitem).
        SELECT COUNT(*)
          FROM yy1_plantrestriction
          WHERE plant = _salesdocumentitem-productionplant
          AND username = _actual_user_alias.

        IF sy-subrc <> 0.
            DATA(lv_error_message) = |You are not allowed to use plant| & | | & |{ lv_salesdocumentitem-productionplant }|.
            APPEND VALUE #( messagetype = 'E' messagetext = lv_error_message ) to messages.
            EXIT.
        ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

 

 

 

After all of the configuration, then you could verify it in the system. We use Yilin as a login user.

Then use this user to create a sales order with plant 1710, you will get the error message you write in the BAdI.

 

Additional Hint:
If you want to receive the error message immediately after adding or changing an item and not only at save, you could implement similar logic in BAdI SD_SLS_CHECK_ITEM.

entry.title: How to Add a Dynamic Custom Section with Custom Fields to the "Manage Sales Orders - Version 2" App
entry.content:
Overview

In this blog post, we will show how to add an additional section called Custom Fields to the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app. This section appears dynamically based on the sales order data. For this blog post, we will use the sales organization as criterion, but you can also implement the logic based on other standard or custom fields.

We use both key user extensibility and developer extensibility for the implementation:

Key User Extensibility

1. In the Custom Fields app, we create a calculated field to manage the visibility of the custom section.

Developer Extensibility

2. In ABAP Development Tools (ADT), we use the Extensibility Wizard to create several custom fields.

3. We adjust the CDS annotation of the newly created extension view for C_SALESORDERMANAGE.

Testing

4. In the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app, we check if the implementation works.

Let's get started!

 

Implementation Steps

1. Creating a Calculated Field

  1. In the Custom Fields app, choose Data Source Extensions, choose New, and enter the following information:
  2. In the detail view of the data source extension, enter a BAdI implementation name, and add a new calculated field of type Boolean.
  3. Choose Create.
  4. Choose Save and then Publish. 
  5. When the data source extension is published, navigate back to the details of the newly created data source extension. You will see a link to the implementation of the calculated field:
  6. Choose the link to open the code editor and enter the following code sample:

 

LOOP AT it_entity_data REFERENCE INTO DATA(lr_entity_data).
 READ TABLE ct_calculated_fields REFERENCE INTO DATA(lr_calculated_fields) INDEX sy-tabix.
   IF lr_entity_data->salesorganization = '1710'.
     lr_calculated_fields->yy1_myuicontrolfield = abap_false.
   ELSE.
     lr_calculated_fields->yy1_myuicontrolfield = abap_true.
   ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

     g. Choose Save and Publish.

As you can see in the code sample, we only want to display the custom fields for sales organization 1710, not for any other sales organizations.

2. Creating Custom Fields

We now create the custom fields that we want to be displayed in the new section. 

  1. Open your ADT and search for the C_SALESORDERMANAGE data definition. Right-click C_SALESORDERMANAGE and select Generate ABAP Repository Objects:
  2. Select ABAP Cloud: Extensibility -> Extension Fields and then choose Next.
  3. Choose a package for your development and choose Next.
  4. In the next step, you can add some custom fields in the first section.
    The second section displays all the extensions that will be created in the business object layer.
    The third section lists all the business object layer extensions that will be created.

    As you started from C_SALESORDERMANAGE, it is pre-selected, but you can also select the OData V4 API for sales orders (A_SALESORDER_2) to create an additional extension for that service. In our scenario, we will focus on the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app (C_SALESORDERMANAGE), and we have created two custom fields with standard data elements. You could also create new data elements in advance and use them here.
  5. Choose Next. A preview of the artifacts to be created will be displayed: 
  6. Choose Next, select a transport request, and then choose Finish. The artifacts are created, and you can find them in your package in the Project Explorer.

3. Adjusting the CDS Annotation

  1. Open the extension for C_SALESORDERMANAGE and adjust the content as follows:

 

 

extend view entity C_SalesOrderManage with
{
  .facet: [ { id: 'CustomFieldsSection',
                 label: 'Custom Fields',                 
                 type: #COLLECTION,
                 position: 65,
                 importance: #HIGH,
                 hidden: #(YY1_MyUIControlField) },
               { id: 'CustomFieldsLeft',
                 label: 'Custom Fields Left',
                 type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,
                 parentId: 'CustomFieldsSection',
                 importance: #HIGH,
                 position: 10,
                 targetQualifier: 'CustomFieldsLeft' },
               { id: 'CustomFieldsRight',
                 label: 'Custom Fields Right',
                 type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,
                 parentId: 'CustomFieldsSection',
                 importance: #HIGH,
                 position: 20,
                 targetQualifier: 'CustomFieldsRight' } ]

  .fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'CustomFieldsLeft', position: 10, importance: #HIGH } ]
  @EndUserText.label: 'My Custom Field 1'
  SalesOrder.ZZ_CUSTOM_FIELD1_SDH as ZZ_CUSTOM_FIELD1_SDH,

  .fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'CustomFieldsRight', position: 10, importance: #HIGH } ]
  @EndUserText.label: 'My Custom Field 2'
  SalesOrder.ZZ_CUSTOM_FIELD2_SDH as ZZ_CUSTOM_FIELD2_SDH
}

 

 

Tips:

  • Line 6: We have chosen position 65 to place the custom fields between the Texts and Status and Blocks sections on the UI. You can find all positions of the other sections in C_SALESORDERMANAGE (Metadata Extension) if you want to have the custom fields in a different position.
  • Line 8: The calculated field name YY1_MyUIControlField is case-sensitive.
  • Line 8: Do not use spaces inside the brackets.
  • Lines 3, 12, 19: To create subsections, assign them via the parentId.
  • Lines 15, 22, 24, 26, 28, 30: To assign custom fields to a specific facet, use the (target)Qualifier.

4. Checking Your Implementation

In the Manage Sales Order - Version 2 app, create a sales order for sales organization 1710 and check if the Custom Fields section is displayed as follows:

Let's create a second sales order for sales organization 1010 and check if the Custom Fields section is displayed.

You can see from the above screenshot that the Custom Fields section is not displayed for sales organization 1010.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK: How to R/W values from/to editable DataTable...
entry.content: Unfortunately, .getChanges() returns an empty array

entry.title: Is it possible to maintain WBS element lv 2 to be revenue collector? on SAP S/4Hana Cloud (Grow)
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

I need some insight after some tries that i tried on the systems. Appreciate the help!

My first question is that :

Scenarios : 

WBS LV 1 - ABC -------> Profile : Project with revenue

Because the profile is project with revenue, we could generate SO to this level of WBS

WBS LV 2 - ABC - 01 ------> WBS Element

After double checking it grey out the billing element parameter as 'NO' 

- So does it means that we cant assign this WBS element to SO and can only be assigned to order which costs based (PR,PO,ProdO,WO)?

- then because every demand of this project is triggered by WBS LV 2, it means that material stock is locked into that project and another question pops up how can we PGI from this project material to created outbound delivery that generated by SO- WBS LV 1 (because we cant assign SO to WBS LV 2)?

- is it ideal that we need to TP project to Project first to move it from WBS LV 2 to WBS LV 1 and consume to SO?

- and does it means that, every specific WBS only can be 1 collector? either it is as Revenue collector or as a Cost collector?

Here is the attachment for your contexts :

 

This WBS element cannot be assigned. Message no. V2515

 

Appreciate if the PS consultant could give me insights about it, Thanks!

Regards,
Anjastra

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Cloud - BAS issue when fiori app is deployed...
entry.content:
Hi,
for me worked add to manifest.json: builder -> customTasks -> configuration -> target:
....

update-existing: true    <- this parameter
....
 
Save file.
Again deploy application: npm run build && fiori deploy --config ui5-deploy.yaml && rimraf archive.zip
and then do undeploy: nom run build && fiori undeploy --config ui5-deploy.yaml

Now you should have clean package. 
Have a great day :))

entry.title: Re: Setting Up Programs in the LMS
entry.content:
Hi John, 
The programs, up to now, cannot be imported via connector or any other import feature, they need to be created manually.

Regards,

Cristina

entry.title: Re: Delta accurals & Delta Settlement

entry.title: Re: Where to see all badges earned? I can see my certificates here https://learning.sap.com/my-learn
entry.content: Hello @AkumaryafirAk, on which SAP website would you like to see your badges? And badges from which properties?

entry.title: Re: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
entry.content:
Hi @nageshcaparthy ,

Thanks for your advice. I decided to make a clean configuration, so I deattached all of the subscriptions and services and deleted test subaccount for Joule. After this I successfully configured the Joule based on the configuration explained in this blog post.

Once again many thanks. 

Best regards,

Tomasz

entry.title: Performance History Block - Rating Scales
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I'm looking for any insights on how to display ratings in People profile > Performance History Block.

 

Currently, we have 2 employee groups in the system, and each group is using different rating scales on the form. From our understanding, ratings displayed in People Profile > Performance History block is configured in Processes and Forms page > Rating Scale and the system allows selecting 1 rating scale to display form ratings on the employee's profile. This causes an issue for the other employee group which is not using the same rating scale on their performance forms, because inaccurate ratings are displayed in their profiles.

Has anyone had a similar case? How did you guys manage this?

entry.title: Re: Can SAP PDMI alone move EBOM from Teamcenter to SAP
entry.content: So then do we need SAP PLM also to be used in this integration?

entry.title: Re: [BTP Workzone] Metadata not found 404
entry.content: I can open the application in the build work zone but I get the error "metadata not found". I developed my application with Cap Model and Custom SAPUI5, it works fine locally. It was successfully deployed to Cloud Foundry. It is also visible in the Html5 Application Repository. However, as I said, I get the error "metadata not found". The examples I have seen so far have generally been made using Fiori elements, but I could not find an example made using Custom SAPUI5.

entry.title: Any solution?
SAP Web IDE personal edition installation files
entry.content: I want to download SAP Web IDE personal edition installation files, and how can i get the url of download

entry.title: Re: Partial transfer of finished goods to another plant f...
entry.content: @ademt1414 Hello Thank you for responding. Inspection lots will be created at both plants. How should we handle this in this case?
